title: Southern Buttermilk Fried Chicken

seo.title: Southern Buttermilk Fried Chicken Recipe - Crispy & Juicy
seo.metaDescription: Master the art of Southern Buttermilk Fried Chicken! Get our easy recipe for crispy, juicy, and flavorful chicken, just like grandma used to make. Perfect for any meal.

seo.h1: The Best Southern Buttermilk Fried Chicken Recipe
h2Headings: What Makes Southern Buttermilk Fried Chicken So Special?
Step-by-Step Instructions for Perfect Fried Chicken
Pro Tips for the Crispiest Southern Fried Chicken
seo.imageAlt: Plate of golden-brown Southern Buttermilk Fried Chicken with a side of mashed potatoes and gravy.
seo.imageCaption: Crispy, juicy, and full of flavor – this Southern Buttermilk Fried Chicken recipe is a true taste of the South!

description: Southern Buttermilk Fried Chicken is a beloved American classic, renowned for its incredibly crispy skin and unbelievably juicy, tender meat. The secret lies in the buttermilk brine, which tenderizes the chicken and infuses it with tangy flavor before it's dredged in a seasoned flour mixture and fried to golden perfection. This iconic dish brings comfort and flavor to any table, embodying the heart of Southern hospitality.

ingredients.notes: thighs, drumsticks, breasts, wings, bone-in, skin-on

instructions.title: Marinate the Chicken
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine the buttermilk and hot sauce (if using). Add the chicken pieces, ensuring they are fully submerged. Cover the bowl and refrigerate for at least 4 hours, or preferably overnight, to tenderize the chicken and infuse flavor.

instructions.title: Prepare the Dredging Station
instructions.text: In a shallow dish or large resealable bag, whisk together the flour, cornstarch, salt, black pepper, paprika, garlic powder, onion powder, and cayenne pepper (if using). Make sure all ingredients are well combined.

instructions.title: Coat the Chicken
instructions.text: Remove the chicken from the buttermilk, allowing any excess to drip off. Do not rinse. Working with one piece at a time, dredge each chicken piece thoroughly in the seasoned flour mixture, pressing firmly to ensure a thick, even coating. Place the coated chicken on a wire rack set over a baking sheet and let it rest for 15-20 minutes. This helps the coating adhere better.

instructions.title: Heat the Oil
instructions.text: Pour the vegetable or peanut oil into a large, heavy-bottomed pot or Dutch oven, filling it about 2-3 inches deep. Heat the oil over medium-high heat until it reaches 350°F (175°C). Use a deep-fry thermometer to monitor the temperature accurately.

instructions.title: Fry the Chicken
instructions.text: Carefully place 2-3 pieces of chicken into the hot oil, ensuring not to overcrowd the pot. Fry for about 6-8 minutes per side, or until golden brown and cooked through. The internal temperature should reach 165°F (74°C). Adjust heat as necessary to maintain a consistent oil temperature of 325-350°F (160-175°C).

instructions.title: Drain and Serve
instructions.text: Once cooked, remove the chicken from the oil and place it on a clean wire rack set over a baking sheet to drain any excess oil. Season lightly with a pinch more salt while hot, if desired. Repeat with remaining chicken pieces. Serve hot with your favorite Southern sides like mashed potatoes, collard greens, or biscuits.

tips: For the crispiest skin, ensure the oil temperature is maintained consistently between 325-350°F. Overcrowding the pot will drop the oil temperature and lead to greasy chicken.
Don't skip the resting step after dredging; it's crucial for the flour coating to adhere properly and prevent it from flaking off during frying.
Leftover fried chicken can be stored in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days. Reheat in an oven or air fryer for best results to restore crispiness.
article: Southern Buttermilk Fried Chicken is a beloved American Chicken Recipes dish enjoyed across American. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
